Core foundations for BYOC Direct Connect advanced across carrier onboarding, Media Edge milestones, fraud protection, and Williams Sonoma go-live readiness, though EU rollout and staging face dependency blocks. On track. Team working with Bandwidth to finalize contract negotiations by 5/22

programs.health_comments:
06/03/2026 🟢 On Track: The program is progressing steadily across three active projects. Core scaling, SBC migration, DTMF propagation, multi-vendor webhook ingestion, and Trust epics are all actively executing with no reported blockers. The VPOP Disaster Recovery project has been placed on hold due to competing priorities introduced mid-264. No items currently require exec escalation.

5/26:Three of four projects are on track — Scale & Concurrency (SBC upgrades, DTMF masking, 10K load testing), Native Stack Enhancements (DTMF propagation, multi-vendor webhook ingestion, BSUID testing), and Voice Observability (telemetry, bug fixes in review) — while VPOP Disaster Recovery (Phase 1) is on hold due to competing AFV priorities introduced mid-264.
5/19  - On Track across all four of its active workstreams.  Scale, Concurrency and Platform Improvements for Native Voice Stack project, which successfully passed its core 10K scaling load tests (UDP/RTP) with 10 of 24 epics actively moving forward and no blockers on its remaining sprints. Foundational work for VPOP Disaster Recovery is on schedule to kick off as soon as the core platform stabilizes, while Voice Observability advanced with one completed epic and ongoing telemetry/diagnostics planning. The Native Stack Enhancements project is progressing smoothly with 4 of its 7 epics—including Native Call Recording, DTMF, Multi-Vendor Webhook, and BSUID—actively under development.
